Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are constructing a Log Skeleton for the given scenario The Log Skeleton is a dictionary with keys equivalence always_before always_after never_together activ_freq directly_follows We are given the activities RO Receive Order CA Check Availability RI Reserve Items PI Pick Items QC Quality Check PO Pack Order GSL Generate Shipping Label D Dispatch IC Invoice Customer RP Record Payment

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to define the constraints based on the typical order fulfillment process

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The process starts with receiving the order RO Then we check availability CA After availability is confirmed we reserve items RI Then we pick the items PI After picking we do a quality check QC Then we pack the order PO Then we generate a shipping label GSL Then we dispatch the order D

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: At some point we invoice the customer IC and then record the payment RP These two are typically after the order is confirmed and might be done in parallel or in sequence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Lets think We can consider that in a normal case each activity occurs exactly once per order

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: But in reality if the process stops at some point for example after CA if the item is not available then not every activity will occur So we must be cautious

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Given the ambiguity I think its safer to leave equivalence empty because we are not given explicit information that the occurrences are always the same

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always Before This constraint says if the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Using the order RO to CA to RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D to IC to RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For CA if CA occurs then RO must have occurred before For RI RI RO and RI CA For PI PI RO PI CA PI RI For QC QC RO QC CA QC RI QC PI For PO PO RO PO CA PO RI PO PI PO QC For GSL GSL RO GSL CA GSL RI GSL PI GSL QC GSL PO For D D RO D CA D RI D PI D QC D PO D GSL For IC IC RO IC CA IC RI IC PI IC QC IC PO IC GSL IC D For RP RP RO RP CA RP RI RP PI RP QC RP PO RP GSL RP D RP IC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always After This constraint is the opposite if the first activity occurs then the second activity is executed in one of the following events

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For RO RO CA RO RI RO PI RO QC RO PO RO GSL RO D RO IC RO RP For CA CA RI CA PI CA QC CA PO CA GSL CA D CA IC CA RP For RI RI PI RI QC RI PO RI GSL RI D RI IC RI RP For PI PI QC PI PO PI GSL PI D PI IC PI RP For QC QC PO QC GSL QC D QC IC QC RP For PO PO GSL PO D PO IC PO RP For GSL GSL D GSL IC GSL RP For D D IC D RP For IC IC RP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Never Together This constraint says that two activities cannot co exist in the same case In a normal process we expect all activities to occur in the same case Therefore we leave the never_together set empty

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We can set for each activity the allowed occurrence counts Since we do not have information we can set a bound that allows 0 or 1 occurrence for each activity because in some cases the activity might not occur if the process stops early and in normal cases it occurs once

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly Follows Constraints This constraint says if the first activity occurs then the second activity shall occur immediately after We can define RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We will represent equivalence as empty always_before as the set of pairs always_after as the set of pairs never_together as empty activ_freq with 0 and 1 for every activity directly_follows as the immediate successor pairs
